title: Fix segfault after assignment pattern XOR error (#6928)
body:
Fix #6928 

When an assignment pattern is used in an unsupported context (e.g., ```'{bit_field: 1'b0} ^ 1'b0```) Verilator reports an UNSUPPORTED error but then segfaults because the ```AstPattern``` node remains in the AST without a dtype, causing null pointer dereference in subsequent passes.

**Changes:**

**src/V3Width.cpp:** Replace malformed ```AstPattern``` node with ```AstConst::BitFalse``` placeholder after reporting the error, following the pattern used in ```methodCallConstraint``` and elsewhere
**test_regress/t/t_pattern_unsup_xor.{v,py,out}:** Add regression test verifying clean error reporting without segfault

**Before:**
```
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: small.v:6:27: Unsupported/Illegal: Assignment pattern member not underneath a supported construct: XOR
Segmentation fault (core dumped)
```

**After:**
```
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: small.v:6:27: Unsupported/Illegal: Assignment pattern member not underneath a supported construct: XOR
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
```
